derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: filteredContacts
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.trigger: Requirements 4, 5: search and sort on contacts list
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Computed via useMemo keyed on search text, sort field, and CONTACTS
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Stored as useState and synced via useEffect watching search/sort

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: filteredMessages
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Computed via useMemo keyed on search text, sort field, selected contact, and MESSAGES
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Stored as useState and synced via useEffect

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: contactMessageDates
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.trigger: Requirement 3: most recent message date per contact
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Pre-computed once via useMemo from MESSAGES (static data)
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Recomputed inline for every contact row on every render

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: statusBarCounts
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Derived from filtered arrays' .length — no separate state needed
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Separate useState for counts, synced via useEffect

performance_signals.why: Filtering 200 contacts and 500 messages on every render is wasteful; useMemo keyed on filter text + sort field avoids recomputation when unrelated state changes
Typing in the Contacts search should not re-render the Messages panel or Detail panel
Callbacks passed to memoized child panels must be stable or memoization is defeated

performance_signals.signal: Stable key prop on list items (using item ID, not index)
performance_signals.why: Filtered lists change which items are shown; index-as-key causes incorrect DOM recycling

performance_signals.signal: No inline object/function creation in map loops
performance_signals.why: Creating new onClick handlers or style objects inside .map() for 200+ items creates GC pressure and defeats memoization of list items

performance_signals.signal: Concurrent rendering for search filtering (useDeferredValue or useTransition)
performance_signals.why: Search inputs filter large lists (200 contacts, 500 messages). Without concurrent rendering, every keystroke blocks the main thread until filtering and re-rendering completes, causing input lag. useDeferredValue on the search text (or useTransition around the filter update) lets React keep the input responsive by deferring the expensive list re-render to a lower priority.
performance_signals.correct: useDeferredValue on the search query, with the deferred value used for filtering/rendering the list. The input is controlled by the immediate state value. Alternatively, useTransition wrapping the state update that triggers filtering.
performance_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: No concurrent rendering at all — input and list update synchronously, causing typing lag
performance_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: Manual debouncing with setTimeout as a substitute for concurrent rendering — this delays the update by a fixed time rather than letting React schedule it intelligently, and shows a gap in React 19 knowledge

performance_signals.signal: Visual feedback during pending transition (stale indicator)
performance_signals.why: Requirements 19-20: while the deferred/transitioned list update is pending, the list should appear dimmed. With useDeferredValue this is detected by comparing the deferred value to the current value (isPending = deferredQuery !== query). With useTransition, the isPending flag is used directly.
performance_signals.correct: Reduced opacity or other visual indicator on the list while the deferred value differs from the current value, or while useTransition's isPending is true
performance_signals.anti_pattern: No visual feedback — the list shows stale results with no indication, or the stale indicator is implemented via a separate useState + useEffect instead of leveraging the transition/deferred API

component_decomposition.composition_opportunity: ContactsPanel and MessagesPanel share a pattern: search input + sort dropdown + scrollable list + count label. A generic ListPanel or FilterableList component (possibly with generics) would reduce duplication.

typescript_signals.signal: Exhaustive switch on message.type for detail rendering
typescript_signals.correct: switch (message.type) with cases for 'text', 'image', 'file' — TypeScript narrows the type in each branch
typescript_signals.anti_pattern: Optional chaining on all fields (message.content?.substring(...)) without narrowing

typescript_signals.correct: FilterableList<T> with typed item renderer: (item: T) => ReactNode
typescript_signals.anti_pattern: items: any[] in the reusable list component

typescript_signals.correct: Typed comparator function signature for each sortable entity

accessibility_checkpoints: List items are keyboard-selectable (button or clickable with role)
Selected item has aria-selected or aria-current
Detail panel updates are perceivable (aria-live or focus management)
